# kmp-nfc

> Kotlin Multiplatform NFC library for Android & iOS — tag reading, NDEF, raw APDU transceive.

## What is kmp-nfc?

kmp-nfc is a Kotlin Multiplatform NFC library. It provides a coroutine-based API for NFC tag communication across Android (API 33+) and iOS (15+) from shared Kotlin code.

Part of the wireless trifecta: kmp-ble (BLE) + kmp-uwb (UWB) + kmp-nfc (NFC), forming the foundation for an Aliro smart lock SDK.

## Installation

Maven Central group: `com.atruedev`

```kotlin
// build.gradle.kts
kotlin {
    sourceSets {
        commonMain.dependencies {
            implementation("com.atruedev:kmp-nfc:0.0.1")
        }
    }
}
```

Android initialization (automatic via AndroidX App Startup):

```kotlin
// Manual init (optional — auto-init is default)
class MyApp : Application() {
    override fun onCreate() {
        super.onCreate()
        KmpNfc.init(this)
    }
}
```

iOS via Swift Package Manager:

```
https://github.com/gary-quinn/kmp-nfc
```

## Core Features

### Tag Discovery

```kotlin
val adapter = NfcAdapter()

adapter.tags(ReaderOptions(alertMessage = "Hold near tag")).collect { tag ->
    tag.use {
        val ndef = it.readNdef()
        println("Records: ${ndef?.records}")
    }
}
```

### NDEF Read/Write

```kotlin
// Read
val ndef = tag.readNdef()
ndef?.records?.forEach { record ->
    when (record) {
        is NdefRecord.Uri -> println("URL: ${record.uri}")
        is NdefRecord.Text -> println("Text: ${record.text} (${record.locale})")
        is NdefRecord.MimeMedia -> println("MIME: ${record.mimeType}")
        is NdefRecord.ExternalType -> println("External: ${record.domain}:${record.externalType}")
        is NdefRecord.Unknown -> println("Unknown TNF: ${record.tnf}")
    }
}

// Write
val message = ndefMessage {
    uri("https://github.com/gary-quinn/kmp-nfc")
    text("Hello NFC", locale = "en")
    mimeMedia("application/json", """{"key": "value"}""".encodeToByteArray())
}
tag.writeNdef(message)
```

### Raw APDU Transceive

```kotlin
// ISO 7816-4 SELECT command
val selectAid = byteArrayOf(0x00, 0xA4.toByte(), 0x04, 0x00, 0x07) +
    byteArrayOf(0xA0.toByte(), 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x04, 0x10, 0x10)
val response = tag.transceive(selectAid)
// response = data + SW1 + SW2

// Parse APDU
val cmd = ApduCommand.fromBytes(rawBytes)
val resp = ApduResponse.success(data = byteArrayOf(0x01))
```

### Adapter State and Capabilities

```kotlin
val adapter = NfcAdapter()

adapter.state.collect { state ->
    when (state) {
        NfcAdapterState.ON -> println("NFC ready")
        NfcAdapterState.OFF -> println("NFC disabled")
        NfcAdapterState.NOT_SUPPORTED -> println("No hardware")
        NfcAdapterState.UNAUTHORIZED -> println("Permission denied")
    }
}

val caps = adapter.capabilities
println("Read NDEF: ${caps.canReadNdef}")
println("Write NDEF: ${caps.canWriteNdef}")
println("Raw transceive: ${caps.canReadRawTag}")
println("Background: ${caps.canBackgroundRead}")
println("Tag types: ${caps.supportedTagTypes}")
```

### Testing Without Hardware

```kotlin
val adapter = FakeNfcAdapter()
val tag = fakeNfcTag {
    identifier(byteArrayOf(0x04, 0x12, 0x34, 0x56))
    type(TagType.ISO_DEP)
    ndef(ndefMessage { uri("https://example.com") })
    onTransceive { command -> byteArrayOf(0x90.toByte(), 0x00) }
    respondAfter(100.milliseconds)
}

// Emit to collectors
adapter.emitTag(tag)

// Error injection
val failingTag = fakeNfcTag { failWith(TagLost()) }

// State simulation
adapter.simulateDisabled()
adapter.simulateEnabled()
```

## Error Handling

```kotlin
try {
    tag.readNdef()
} catch (e: NfcException) {
    when (e.error) {
        is TagLost -> println("Tag moved away")
        is ReadOnly -> println("Cannot write")
        is UnsupportedOperation -> println("Not supported: ${e.error.operation}")
        is TransceiveError -> println("Communication failed")
        else -> println("Error: ${e.error.message}")
    }
}
```

## Architecture

- Cold `Flow<NfcTag>` — collecting starts session, cancelling ends it
- Per-tag serial execution via `limitedParallelism(1)` — no locks or atomics
- Shared NDEF codec in commonMain — both platforms delegate to `parseNdefRecord()`
- Sealed error hierarchy with composable interfaces (`AdapterError`, `TagOperationError`)
- Capability-aware API — platform asymmetry exposed, not hidden

## Links

- GitHub: https://github.com/gary-quinn/kmp-nfc
